topic.what: Verifiable shuffle protocol for ElGamal ciphertexts with O(log N) proof size
topic.how: Combines KZG polynomial commitments for permutation proof with single IPA for consistency, linked via shared commitment to shuffled vector
topic.why_matters: Proof size bottleneck in deployed e-voting and blockchain mix-nets; reduces shuffle proof from O(N) or O(√N) to O(log N) enabling practical large-scale deployments

characteristics.what_it_measures: Communication complexity of the shuffle proof
characteristics.vs_baseline: 1.65x smaller than Hoffmann et al. [28] (4.13 KB); 200x smaller than O(√N) schemes (512 KB); 90000x smaller than O(N) schemes (224 MB)
characteristics.context: N=2^20 ciphertexts; compressed BLS12-381 encoding (48B G1, 32B field)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Computational cost for prover to generate proof
characteristics.vs_baseline: 2x improvement vs Hoffmann et al. (~30N to 15N exponentiations)
characteristics.evidence: Single-threaded benchmark on Apple M3 Pro

characteristics.what_it_measures: Computational cost for verifier to check proof
characteristics.vs_baseline: 1.67x improvement vs Hoffmann et al. (~10N to 6N exponentiations)
characteristics.evidence: Single-threaded benchmark on Apple M3 Pro

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of group exponentiations for prover
Number of group exponentiations for verifier
characteristics.context: Includes 3 pairing computations in permutation check

characteristics.what_itasures: Number of group/field elements in proof
characteristics.vs_baseline: 3x reduction in log N coefficient vs Hoffmann et al. (6 log N + 8 G1 + 4 F)
characteristics.context: Coefficient 2 vs 6 for log N term

characteristics.vs_baseline: Trade-off: transparent setup (Hoffmann et al.) vs structured SRS (this work)

novelty: First implementation benchmarks for O(log N) verifiable shuffle
First O(log N) scheme with proof size coefficient 2 for log N term
Reduces prover/verifier exponentiations by ~2x vs prior O(log N) work
Single-commitment design linking permutation and consistency arguments
Updatable SRS enabling reuse across applications
open_problems: Prover efficiency still O(N) with large constant; 19min for 1M ciphertexts may limit practical adoption
Transparency vs efficiency trade-off not fully resolved (requires SRS trust)
Integration with multi-server mix-nets not addressed
Practical deployment in national elections requires further optimization
